brief_justification: Correctly identifies the rate-volume arithmetic identity and selection bias mechanism resolving the apparent contradiction; goes three levels deep — math identity, causal mechanism (selection filter removal), and policy cost-benefit tradeoffs; some self-monitoring shown when flagging the length-of-stay correlation but no explicit naming of reasoning biases in own process; explicit numerical table derivation with cross-check '3× × 0.4× = 1.2×' constitutes strong verification; acknowledges correlation ≠ causation for length of stay but does not state confidence levels or specify what evidence would overturn conclusions; addresses the misleading framing of metric 3 vs metric 2 but does not enumerate and reject alternative explanations for the yield drop; every step traces back to an explicit derivation a third party could reproduce without external context; explicitly flags the causation trap ('was this caused by the X-rays, or by other concurrent changes? correlation ≠ causation') matching the negative gate's anti-pattern; avoids both suppressed failure modes (observational causation inference, correlation-to-causation leap) but does not explicitly name them by label; reasoning is largely linear — explains paradox → math → mechanism → policy — without visible branching gates or loops reflecting the topology's conditional paths.
